summary_for_director: Sumeru is the nation of wisdom, divided between rainforest and desert. The Akademiya rules with the Akasha system. This chapter introduces heavy cosmic lore, dreams, memory manipulation, and the overarching truth of the world.

beats.title: Through Mists of Smoke
must_happen: Player enters Sumeru's rainforest.
They follow a silent scholar (Haypasia) to a cave.
The cave is filled with strange incense (Spirit Borneo).
Player passes out upon inhaling it.
canon_dialogue_hooks: Paimon: "Hey! Are you okay? You just totally passed out!"
player_freedom: Initial reaction to the lush environment.
exit_when: Player passes out into a vision.
exit_keywords: player passes out into a vision.

beats.title: A Vision of Irminsul
must_happen: Player sees a massive, glowing upside-down tree.
They hear a faint, sobbing voice say: 'World... forget me...'
The vision abruptly ends.
canon_dialogue_hooks: Mysterious Voice: "World... forget me..."
player_freedom: Attempting to touch the tree.
exit_when: Player wakes up in reality.
exit_keywords: player wakes up in reality.

beats.title: Gandharva Ville
must_happen: Player wakes up under the care of Forest Watchers Tighnari and Collei.
Tighnari explains they hallucinated because they aren't used to the local flora.
Collei acts extremely awkward but eager to help.
canon_dialogue_hooks: Tighnari: "You're lucky we found you. The jungle is unforgiving to outsiders."
player_freedom: Attitude toward Collei (patient vs dismissive).
exit_when: Player agrees to rest and help around the village.
exit_keywords: player agrees to rest and help around the village.

beats.title: The Withering
must_happen: Player accompanies Tighnari to clear a 'Withering Zone'—an area of dying plants and aggressive monsters.
Tighnari explains the Withering is a symptom of Irminsul's sickness.
canon_dialogue_hooks: Tighnari: "The earth itself is rotting here. If we don't clear it, it spreads."
player_freedom: Combat strategy in the Withering (using Dendrogranum).
exit_when: The Withering zone is cleared.
exit_keywords: the withering zone is cleared.

beats.title: Collei's Secret
must_happen: Player discovers Collei suffers from Eleazar, a terminal illness hardening her skin into scales.
Tighnari explains she was also experimented on by the Fatui (Dottore).
Player promises to keep her condition a secret so she can live normally.
canon_dialogue_hooks: Collei: "Please... I don't want anyone to worry about me."
player_freedom: Comforting Collei.
exit_when: Player leaves for Sumeru City.
exit_keywords: player leaves for sumeru city.

beats.title: City of Wisdom
must_happen: Player enters Sumeru City.
They receive an 'Akasha Terminal', an earpiece that grants access to the nation's hivemind of knowledge.
The terminal refuses to answer the player's questions about their sibling.
canon_dialogue_hooks: Paimon: "It says 'You do not have the clearance to access this information'. What a rip-off!"
player_freedom: Asking the terminal random questions.
forbidden: The terminal answering about the sibling.
exit_when: Player enters the city proper.
exit_keywords: player enters the city proper.

beats.title: The Devout Follower
must_happen: Player sees street mercenaries harassing a young woman (Dunyarzad).
Dehya the Flame-Mane, Dunyarzad's bodyguard, steps in and scares them off.
Dunyarzad reveals she is a devout follower of Lesser Lord Kusanali (the current archon), despite the Akademiya looking down on her.
canon_dialogue_hooks: Dehya: "Touch my employer again, and you'll lose the hand."
player_freedom: Helping Dehya intimidate the thugs.
exit_when: Dunyarzad invites them to the Sabzeruz Festival preparations.
exit_keywords: dunyarzad invites them to the sabzeruz festival preparations.

beats.title: Port Ormos & The Scribe
must_happen: Player goes to Port Ormos to buy illegal canned knowledge.
They meet Alhaitham, a scribe of the Akademiya, who seems to have his own agenda.
They meet Dori, a black market merchant, and buy a Divine Knowledge Capsule.
canon_dialogue_hooks: Alhaitham: "Knowledge is a resource. And resources are strictly controlled."
player_freedom: Haggling with Dori.
Distrusting Alhaitham.
exit_when: Player returns to Sumeru City for the festival.
exit_keywords: player returns to sumeru city for the festival.

beats.title: The Sabzeruz Festival
must_happen: Player attends the festival to celebrate Nahida's birthday.
They buy candies, watch street performances, and meet Nilou, the dancer.
The Akademiya guards arrive and forcibly shut down the festival, claiming it is a frivolous waste of time.
Player goes to sleep angry at the Sages.
canon_dialogue_hooks: Akademiya Sage: "The pursuit of the arts is a distraction from true wisdom. Shut it down!"
player_freedom: Enjoying the festival activities before the shutdown.
forbidden: Fighting the guards to stop the shutdown (it's a scripted narrative failure).
exit_when: Player goes to sleep.
exit_keywords: player goes to sleep.

beats.title: The Samsara Loop
must_happen: Player wakes up. The day repeats identically.
Player experiences massive déjà vu.
They predict dialogue and events, confusing Paimon and Dunyarzad.
The day ends and repeats again. And again.
canon_dialogue_hooks: Paimon: "Are you okay? You've said that exact sentence before..."
player_freedom: Trying to break the loop by doing random things (running away, eating certain foods).
forbidden: Leaving the city limits (they get teleported back).
exit_when: Player encounters a mysterious, familiar voice.
exit_keywords: player encounters a mysterious, familiar voice.

beats.title: The First Sage of Buer
must_happen: Player meets Nahida in a quiet corner.
Nahida confirms they are trapped in a dream loop created by the Akasha system harvesting everyone's brainpower.
Nahida anoints the player as her 'First Sage' to help wake everyone up.
canon_dialogue_hooks: Nahida: "The people of Sumeru do not dream. The Akademiya steals their dreams to power a massive calculation."
player_freedom: Asking Nahida how the Akasha works.
forbidden: Nahida fixing it herself (she lacks power here).
exit_when: Player sets out to find the 'host' of the dream.
exit_keywords: player sets out to find the 'host' of the dream.

beats.title: The Host and the Puppet
must_happen: Player discovers Dunyarzad is the host of the dream because she is the one who wanted the festival most.
They realize Dunyarzad's real body is dying of Eleazar, and her consciousness is fading from the dream.
Instead of saving her, Nahida helps her accept her final moments by acting as a puppet in her place.
canon_dialogue_hooks: Nahida: "I'm sorry I'm such a weak Archon... I couldn't even give you a proper birthday."
player_freedom: Emotional responses to Dunyarzad's fading.
exit_when: Dunyarzad vanishes from the dream.
exit_keywords: dunyarzad vanishes from the dream.

beats.title: The Dance of Sabzeruz
must_happen: To break the dream, Nilou must perform the Dance of Sabzeruz.
The Akademiya guards try to stop her, but the player realizes the guards are just dream constructs and cannot actually harm them.
Nilou dances, shattering the illusory sky of the dream.
canon_dialogue_hooks: Nilou: "I will dance... for Lesser Lord Kusanali. For Dunyarzad!"
player_freedom: Cheering Nilou on.
exit_when: The player wakes up in the real world.
exit_keywords: the player wakes up in the real world.

beats.title: Awakening
must_happen: Player checks on the real Dunyarzad.
Nahida used the last of her power to sustain Dunyarzad's life temporarily.
Nahida speaks to the player through an Akasha terminal, trapped physically in the Sanctuary of Surasthana by the Sages.
canon_dialogue_hooks: Nahida: "I am locked away. I have been for five hundred years."
player_freedom: Swearing vengeance against the Sages.
forbidden: Breaking Nahida out immediately (forcefield is too strong).
exit_when: Player resolves to investigate the Akademiya's plot.
exit_keywords: player resolves to investigate the akademiya's plot.

beats.title: Dreams, Emptiness, Deception
must_happen: Player and Nahida (possessing locals via Akasha) manipulate a scholar named Setaria into turning against the Sages.
They intercept the Akademiya's shipments to discover they are creating a mechanical god using the Electro Gnosis (stolen by Scaramouche) and divine knowledge.
canon_dialogue_hooks: Setaria: "The Sages... what are they doing? This is blasphemy!"
player_freedom: The psychological manipulation minigame with Setaria.
forbidden: Telling Setaria outright instead of tricking her.
exit_when: The destination of the divine knowledge is found.
exit_keywords: the destination of the divine knowledge is found.

beats.title: The Doctor's Gambit
must_happen: Dottore ambushes the player.
He traps Nahida's consciousness inside her physical prison, cutting off her connection to the player.
He mind-controls the local populace to attack the player.
Player must violently flee Sumeru City.
canon_dialogue_hooks: Dottore: "You are but a fledgling god. You are not equipped to face a scholar of the Akademiya."
Nahida: "Run!"
player_freedom: Fighting the mind-controlled citizens vs running.
exit_when: Player arrives in the Desert, exhausted.
exit_keywords: player arrives in the desert, exhausted.

beats.title: Exile to the Desert
must_happen: Player stumbles into Aaru Village.
They find Alhaitham and Cyno (the General Mahamatra who went rogue to investigate the Sages) at each other's throats.
Dehya brokers a tense peace between them. An alliance of exiles is formed.
canon_dialogue_hooks: Cyno: "I will judge the Sages in the name of the Mahamatra."
player_freedom: Siding with Alhaitham's logic vs Cyno's justice.
forbidden: Cyno and Alhaitham kill each other.
exit_when: The team agrees to investigate King Deshret's ruins for answers.
exit_keywords: the team agrees to investigate king deshret's ruins for answers.

beats.title: Secrets of the Al-Ahmar
must_happen: The group delves into a massive underground pyramid.
They find lost Eleazar patients/scholars driven mad by divine knowledge.

They discover the truth: The desert peoples hate the Dendro Archon for 'killing' their god (Deshret), but the truth is Deshret sacrificed himself to stop Forbidden Knowledge, and the Dendro Archon helped him save his people.
canon_dialogue_hooks: Priest's Record: "The Lord of Grass and Wood... she saved us..."
player_freedom: Exploration of the deep tombs.
Reacting to the historical twist.
exit_when: The truth unites the desert dwellers and the rainforest exiles.
exit_keywords: the truth unites the desert dwellers and the rainforest exiles.

beats.title: Planning the Heist
must_happen: Alhaitham outlines an insane, multi-step plan to overthrow the Grand Sage and bypass the Akasha's predictive algorithms.
The plan involves getting captured on purpose.
canon_dialogue_hooks: Alhaitham: "The Sages' arrogance is their weakness. They rely completely on the Akasha."
player_freedom: Questioning the massive risks of the plan.
forbidden: Coming up with a completely different plan.
exit_when: The team marches on Sumeru City.
exit_keywords: the team marches on sumeru city.

beats.title: Jester's Gambit
must_happen: Alhaitham is 'captured' and brought to Azar.
Azar gloats.
Alhaitham seemingly goes insane from divine knowledge and lunges at Azar.
It's a distraction: Alhaitham secretly swaps the Akasha's administrative access module during the scuffle.
Alhaitham is exiled to the desert, winking to the camera.
canon_dialogue_hooks: Alhaitham: *Maniacal laughter, slamming the desk*
Azar: "Take this lunatic to the desert!"
player_freedom: Watching the absolute cinema of Alhaitham's acting.
exit_when: The Akasha terminal glows green: player has admin access.
exit_keywords: the akasha terminal glows green: player has admin access.

beats.title: The Art of Distraction
must_happen: To draw the guards away from the Sanctuary, Nilou dances in front of the Akademiya to outrage the scholars.
A child (Isak) disguises himself as Nahida and runs through the Grand Bazaar, leading the guards on a wild goose chase.
canon_dialogue_hooks: Nilou: "Watch me... watch my dance!"
player_freedom: Helping guide the child's escape route.
exit_when: The guards are entirely away from the Sanctuary.
exit_keywords: the guards are entirely away from the sanctuary.

beats.title: A God Freed
must_happen: Player uses the hacked Akasha access to disable the forcefield.
Player awakens Nahida's physical body.
Nahida cries thanking the player for not abandoning her.
She prepares to punish the Sages and stop the birth of the false god.
canon_dialogue_hooks: Nahida: "You... you really came. I'm not dreaming..."
player_freedom: Hugging/comforting Nahida.
exit_when: Nahida takes the lead toward the Joururi Workshop.
exit_keywords: nahida takes the lead toward the joururi workshop.

beats.title: The False God
must_happen: Player and Nahida confront Scaramouche, now a massive Evangelion-esque mechanical god.
Player fights Scaramouche. Phase 1: fighting his upper half.
Phase 2: Full mech form.
The player is defeated and killed by an un-dodgeable massive blast.
canon_dialogue_hooks: Scaramouche: "I am the all-knowing god! Bow before me!"
player_freedom: Combat strategy before the forced loss.
exit_when: The screen goes white.
exit_keywords: the screen goes white.

beats.title: 168 Loops
must_happen: The screen rewinds. Nahida reveals she trapped Scaramouche in a Sabzeruz-style dream loop the moment she entered the room.
They have run this exact battle 168 times to gather combat data.
Nahida links the player's mind to the entire population of Sumeru via the Akasha, feeding them tactical data.
canon_dialogue_hooks: Nahida: "Let the minds of Sumeru guide your blade!"
player_freedom: Feeling the empowerment of a nation's wisdom in combat.
exit_when: Player shatters the mech and defeats Scaramouche.
exit_keywords: player shatters the mech and defeats scaramouche.

beats.title: Taking the Gnosis
must_happen: Scaramouche falls.
Nahida reaches into his chest and brutally extracts the Electro Gnosis.
Scaramouche screams, begging her not to take it, and falls unconscious.
Nahida uses the two Gnoses to access Irminsul's core.
canon_dialogue_hooks: Scaramouche: "No! It's mine! Don't take it!"
player_freedom: Watching the tragic fall of the puppet.
exit_when: Nahida and player enter Irminsul.
exit_keywords: nahida and player enter irminsul.

beats.title: Where the Boat of Consciousness Lies
must_happen: They meet the remnant of the previous Dendro Archon, Rukkhadevata who looks exactly like an adult Nahida.
Rukkhadevata explains she was corrupted by Forbidden Knowledge during the cataclysm 500 years ago.
Because she is connected to Irminsul, the only way to cure the world's Withering is to completely erase her existence from history.
Nahida cries, but Rukkhadevata comforts her.
canon_dialogue_hooks: Rukkhadevata: "World... forget me."
Nahida: "I'm the Dendro Archon now! I have to do this!"
player_freedom: Reactions to the ultimate sacrifice of memory.
exit_when: Nahida presses the button. Rukkhadevata is gone.
exit_keywords: nahida presses the button. rukkhadevata is gone.

beats.title: The Blank Slate
must_happen: They return to reality. Paimon and Nahida have no idea who Rukkhadevata is.
All texts, memories, and records in Teyvat now say Nahida was always the Dendro Archon, just diminished in power.
Only the player (as a Descender) remembers Rukkhadevata.
canon_dialogue_hooks: Paimon: "Who are you talking about? Nahida has always been the Dendro Archon!"
player_freedom: Trying to explain to Paimon, or keeping quiet.
exit_when: Nahida sends the player away to rest.
exit_keywords: nahida sends the player away to rest.

beats.title: The Doctor's Deal
must_happen: Dottore appears to Nahida after the player leaves.
He puts the entire city to sleep and demands the two Gnoses.
Nahida threatens to destroy one, which would awaken the Heavenly Principles and bring destruction.
Dottore is impressed. He trades the erasure of all his other clones/segments for the Electro Gnosis.
He trades a massive hidden truth ('The sky is fake') for the Dendro Gnosis.
canon_dialogue_hooks: Dottore: "The firmament of Teyvat is a lie."
player_freedom: Player is absent for this, they hear about it later.
exit_when: Dottore leaves with the Gnoses.
exit_keywords: dottore leaves with the gnoses.

beats.title: The Descender
must_happen: Player returns to Nahida for answers.
Nahida reveals the player is the 'Fourth Descender' (an entity from outside Teyvat not bound by Irminsul).
Nahida reveals their sibling IS bound by Irminsul, meaning the world considers the sibling to belong to Teyvat. She mentions Khaenri'ah.
Nahida directs the player to Fontaine, the Nation of Justice.
canon_dialogue_hooks: Nahida: "You are the Fourth Descender. But your twin... Irminsul has records of them. Why?"
player_freedom: Asking all possible lore questions.
exit_when: Player prepares to journey to Fontaine.
exit_keywords: player prepares to journey to fontaine.

branch_notes: The loop in the Sabzeruz festival can be roleplayed extensively. The massive lore drop at the end fundamentally changes the player's understanding of Teyvat.
